---
title: "Core Concepts in Realistic Evaluation"
description: "Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Realistic Evaluation."
book_id: "realistic-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Synthesis"
citation_key: "pawson_tilley_1997_realistic_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:realistic-evaluation:introduction:c0011"
  - "chunk:realistic-evaluation:ch03-chapter-3-in-with-the-new-introducing-scientific-realism:c0051"
  - "chunk:realistic-evaluation:ch09-chapter-9-the-new-rules-of-realistic-evaluation:c0008"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This synthesis draws across the generated notes for *Realistic Evaluation* [@pawson_tilley_1997_realistic_evaluation].

## Central Logic

Realist evaluation studies programs through context-mechanism-outcome configurations rather than asking only whether a whole program worked.

## Concepts

- Context-mechanism-outcome configuration: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Realist research design: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Theory testing: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Outcome patterns: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Subgroup variation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Teacher-learner cycle: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.

## Links

- [Reader's Guide to Context, Mechanism, and Outcome](section-01-reader-guide-cmo.qmd)
- [Scientific Realism and CMO Hypotheses](section-02-scientific-realism-and-cmo.qmd)
- [The New Rules and CMO Configurations](section-03-new-rules-cmo-configurations.qmd)

## References
